prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
Here are four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler:

"In the year of our Lord two thousand and twenty, a mystical entity known as 'Salesforce' didst reveal unto me the secrets of its quarterly report, wherein it was written that the company hadst conjured forth $1.1 billion in revenue from the ether, a sum so great that it didst rival the treasure of the Great Khan. And lo, the entity's 'gross margin' didst increase by 74.6%, a feat so wondrous that it didst make my quill hand tremble with awe. Meanwhile, the 'customer acquisition cost' didst dwindle to a mere 3.3 times the cost of a noble steed, a bargain so fantastical that I didst wonder if the entity's accountants were indeed possessed by the spirits of arithmetic. And yet, despite these marvels, the entity's 'net retention rate' didst hover at a mere 125%, a figure so confounding that I didst fear I hadst misinterpreted the sacred runes of the spreadsheet."
